questions.question: In HTML, which attribute should be unique on a page and is commonly used as a stable locator for automation?

questions.explanation: git checkout -b <branch> creates the branch and checks it out in one step.
questions.question: Which Git command creates a new local branch named 'feature/login' and switches to it?

questions.question: In Maven, which file typically defines project dependencies and build configuration?

questions.explanation: GET is used to read/retrieve resources and should be safe and idempotent.
questions.question: Which HTTP method is most appropriate to retrieve a resource without modifying server state?

, which CSS selector targets the Save button most robustly for automation?

questions.explanation: pytest parametrization runs a test function multiple times with different argument values, enabling data-driven testing.
questions.question: In pytest, which feature is commonly used to run the same test logic with multiple input datasets?

questions.explanation: POM centralizes selectors and UI actions to reduce duplication and improve maintainability when the UI changes.

options.A: Store test data in external files (CSV/JSON)
options.B: Encapsulate page locators and page interactions behind methods
options.C: Execute tests in parallel across browsers
options.D: Generate HTML reports for the test run

questions.question: In the Page Object Model (POM), what is the primary responsibility of a Page Object class?
questions.topic: Test Design & Automation Patterns (Page Object Model, Data-Driven)

questions.explanation: Condition-based explicit waits and isolating state address timing and concurrency issues directly; sleeps and retries often hide problems and slow pipelines.

options.A: Add fixed sleeps (e.g., 10 seconds) before every click
options.B: Replace UI tests with API tests only
options.C: Use explicit waits for specific conditions (e.g., visible + enabled) and avoid shared test state
options.D: Rerun failed tests up to 5 times and ignore failures if they pass on retry

questions.question: A CI pipeline runs UI tests in parallel and intermittently fails with errors like 'element not clickable' and 'timeout waiting for selector'. Which change is most likely to reduce flakiness without masking real defects?

questions.explanation: Modular design with stable interfaces and configuration improves maintainability; pinning versions and documenting licenses supports reliable builds and compliant use of open-source software.

options.A: Hard-code device addresses and timing values directly in the control logic for simplicity
options.B: Create modular components with clear interfaces, configuration-driven parameters, and version-pinned dependencies with documented licenses
options.C: Copy-paste open-source code into the project to avoid dependency management
options.D: Allow each developer to choose their own library versions to speed up development

questions.question: You are designing reusable automation components for a production line cell (sensors + actuators). Which approach best supports maintainability and safe operation when integrating open-source libraries?

questions.explanation: A tolerance of ±0.1 mm means the acceptable range is 50.0 mm minus 0.1 and plus 0.1, i.e., 49.9–50.1 mm.

options.A: The distance must be exactly 50.0 mm with no deviation
options.B: Any distance between 49.9 mm and 50.1 mm is acceptable
options.C: Any distance between 49.0 mm and 51.0 mm is acceptable
options.D: The tolerance applies only to the hole diameter, not the spacing

questions.question: A technical drawing for an automated fixture shows a hole pattern with a center-to-center distance of 50 ± 0.1 mm. When using technical drawing software to prepare a manufacturing-ready model, what is the correct interpretation?
